steps.action_input: [retriever] [doc_04 | score=0.747] Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) combines a retrieval component with a generative model. Given a query, a retriever fetches relevant passages from an external co
steps.observation:
Here is a 2-3 sentence summary of the text:

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) combines retrieval and generative models to produce relevant passages. Evaluating retrievals involves metrics like Precision@k, Recall@k, and Mean Reciprocal Rank. Techniques such as instruction tuning and causal self-attention aim to improve model performance and reduce hallucination in low-resource languages.

final_answer:
The key ideas behind Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) are:

1. Combining a retrieval component with a generative model to produce relevant passages.
2. Evaluating retrievals using metrics such as Precision@k, Recall@k, and Mean Reciprocal Rank.
3. Applying techniques like instruction tuning and causal self-attention to improve performance and reduce hallucination.
